data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file to disk but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so the C# compiler never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `<Compile>` in the target.
data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but the compiler doesn't know about it.

The fix is to add an `<ItemGroup>` within the target that includes the generated file in the `Compile` items:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

This needs to be added after the file is written.

{"path": "/tmp/sv-61fa868272fd4cb58905d16c25160fd4/TestProject.csproj", "old_str": "    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>", "new_str": "    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
  </Target>"}

@@ -60,6 +60,10 @@
       Configuration="$(Configuration)"
       TargetFramework="$(TargetFramework)" />
 
+    <ItemGroup>
+      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
+    </ItemGroup>
+
     <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
   </Target>

data.content: Build succeeds now. The problem was that the `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never told MSBuild to compile it. Adding `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target registers the generated file with the C# compiler.
